preview_text:
The model favors Japan at 71%, assigning Tunisia only an 8% chance of victory. Japan emerges as a clear favorite in this matchup, though the model's probability reflects a decisive advantage rather than overwhelming dominance.

The draw sits at 21% in the model, suggesting the match carries meaningful competitive balance beyond the win probabilities alone. The gap between Japan's 71% and Tunisia's 8% indicates the model expects a pronounced disparity, yet the 21% draw probability indicates that a stalemate remains plausible. This spread implies Japan should control possession and territory but that Tunisia is not forecast to be completely overmatched.

A notable divergence separates the model from sportsbook consensus. The books assess Japan at 55% and Tunisia at 18%—roughly 16 percentage points wider in Japan's favor than the model's estimate. The draw sits near parity between sources (model 21%, consensus 27%), while Polymarket prices (57% Japan, 17% Tunisia) align more closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct; the difference warrants attention as bettors compare offerings.
